structured_payload_candidate.title: Recommendation for reheat when minimum ventilation is high
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the minimum ventilation rate is more than 25% of the cooling maximum, or DCV is used, a reheat box is recommended to avoid overcooling. Additionally, DCV logic is not provided for cooling-only boxes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Minimum ventilation rate > 25% of Vcool-max, or DCV is used
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Use a reheat box instead of cooling-only terminal unit.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the minimum ventilation rate is more than 25% or so of the cooling maximum, or DCV is used, a reheat box is recommended to avoid overcooling. DCV logic is not provided for cooling-only boxes, because doing so results in periods of overcooling, as the CO2 levels due to occupants rises much faster than the cooling load due to occupants because of thermal mass.

evidence_quote: If the minimum ventilation rate is more than 25% or so of the cooling maximum, or DCV is used, a reheat box is recommended to avoid overcooling. DCV logic is not provided for cooling-only boxes, because doing so results in periods of overcooling, as the CO2 levels due to occupants rises much faster than the cooling load due to occupants because of thermal mass.
evidence_text: If the minimum ventilation rate is more than 25% or so of the cooling maximum, or DCV is used, a reheat box is recommended to avoid overcooling. DCV logic is not provided for cooling-only boxes, because doing so results in periods of overcooling, as the CO2 levels due to occupants rises much faster than the cooling load due to occupants because of thermal mass.
judge_reason: Operational recommendation based on minimum ventilation and DCV.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating capability condition for cooling-only terminals
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Cooling-only terminal units can provide heating only when the AHU supply air temperature is more than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: AHU supply air temperature > room temperature + 3°C (5°F)
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Heating is allowed; otherwise heating airflow setpoint is limited to minimum endpoint (see 5.5.5.3 a).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Cooling-only terminal units can provide heating only when the AHU supply air temperature is more than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature.

evidence_quote: Cooling-only terminal units can provide heating only when the AHU supply air temperature is more than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature.
evidence_text: Cooling-only terminal units can provide heating only when the AHU supply air temperature is more than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature.
judge_reason: Operational condition for heating by cooling-only terminals.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow alarm threshold determination
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The constant value thresholds (50 cfm, 10% of Vcool-max) should be determined based on the minimum accuracy of the airflow transducer and A/D converter combination.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set alarm thresholds based on transducer/ADC minimum accuracy.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.

evidence_quote: The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
evidence_text: The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
judge_reason: Application guidance on threshold determination for airflow alarms.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Commissioning override switches
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Provide software switches interlocked to a system-level point to force zone airflow setpoint to zero, Vcool-max, Vmin, force damper full closed/open, and reset request-hours accumulator.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Commissioning mode activation via system-level point
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Switches force specific override states for testing.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Check leaking dampers by forcing all VAV boxes closed and recording airflow at AHU.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.5.7. Testing/Commissioning Overrides. Provide software switches that interlock to a system-level point to a. force zone airflow setpoint to zero, b. force zone airflow setpoint to Vcool-max, c. force zone airflow setpoint to Vmin, d. force damper full closed/open, and e. reset request-hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the next section).

evidence_quote: 5.5.7. Testing/Commissioning Overrides. Provide software switches that interlock to a system-level point to a. force zone airflow setpoint to zero, b. force zone airflow setpoint to Vcool-max, c. force zone airflow setpoint to Vmin, d. force damper full closed/open, and e. reset request-hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the next section).
evidence_text: 5.5.7. Testing/Commissioning Overrides. Provide software switches that interlock to a system-level point to a. force zone airflow setpoint to zero, b. force zone airflow setpoint to Vcool-max, c. force zone airflow setpoint to Vmin, d. force damper full closed/open, and e. reset request-hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the next section).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low airflow Level 3 alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate a Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Measured airflow < 50% of setpoint, for 10 minutes continuously, and setpoint > 0
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.
evidence_text: b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule for low airflow Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Suppression of low airflow alarms for zones with importance multiplier 0
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If a zone has an importance multiplier of 0 for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms are suppressed for that zone.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone importance multiplier = 0 for static pressure reset T&R loop
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress low airflow alarms for that zone.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: c. If a zone has an importance multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

evidence_quote: c. If a zone has an importance multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
evidence_text: c. If a zone has an importance multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
judge_reason: Alarm suppression condition based on importance multiplier.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow sensor calibration fault
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of Vcool-max or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fan OFF, and airflow sensor reading > max(0.1*Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Airflow sensor reading high while fan is off
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
evidence_text: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule for airflow sensor calibration.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Leaking damper detection
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If damper position is 0% and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of Vcool-max or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while fan is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Damper position = 0%, airflow sensor reading > max(0.1*Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 10 minutes, fan proven ON
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Leaking damper (flow detected when damper closed)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule for leaking damper detection.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow setpoint mapping in cooling state
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the zone state is cooling, map the cooling-loop output to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature is greater than room temperature, limit the setpoint to no higher than the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Active airflow setpoint = mapping(cooling-loop output, range: [minimum endpoint, cooling maximum endpoint]). If SAT > room temp, cap at minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.5.5.1. When the Zone State is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. a. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.

evidence_quote: 5.5.5.1. When the Zone State is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. a. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
evidence_text: 5.5.5.1. When the Zone State is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. a. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
judge_reason: Airflow setpoint mapping logic for cooling state.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow setpoint in deadband state
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the zone state is deadband, the active airflow setpoint is set to the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.5.5.2. When the Zone State is deadband, the active airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.

evidence_quote: 5.5.5.2. When the Zone State is deadband, the active airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
evidence_text: 5.5.5.2. When the Zone State is deadband, the active airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
judge_reason: Airflow setpoint behavior in deadband state.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow setpoint mapping in heating state
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the zone state is heating, map the heating-loop output to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the heating maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature is less than 3°C (5°F) above room temperature, limit the setpoint to no higher than the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Active airflow setpoint = mapping(heating-loop output, range: [minimum endpoint, heating maximum endpoint]). If SAT < room temp + 3°C, cap at minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.5.5.3. When the Zone State is heating, the Heating Loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the heating maximum endpoint. a. If supply air temperature from the air handler is less than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.

evidence_quote: 5.5.5.3. When the Zone State is heating, the Heating Loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the heating maximum endpoint. a. If supply air temperature from the air handler is less than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
evidence_text: 5.5.5.3. When the Zone State is heating, the Heating Loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the heating maximum endpoint. a. If supply air temperature from the air handler is less than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
judge_reason: Airflow setpoint mapping logic for heating state.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VAV damper modulation for airflow control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The VAV damper is modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured airflow at the active setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Modulate damper to match measured airflow to active setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.5.5.4. The VAV damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured airflow at the active setpoint.

evidence_quote: 5.5.5.4. The VAV damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured airflow at the active setpoint.
evidence_text: 5.5.5.4. The VAV damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured airflow at the active setpoint.
judge_reason: VAV damper modulation for airflow control.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low airflow Level 4 alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate a Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Measured airflow < 70% of setpoint, for 10 minutes continuously, and setpoint > 0
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
judge_reason: Fault diagnostic rule for low airflow Level 4 alarm.
